name: one-time-secret
title: One-Time Secret
description: Allows secrets to be transmitted using the One-Time Secret website.
author: larsasolidor

preferences.name: region
preferences.title: Data centre region
preferences.description: Secrets are stored only in the region you select. Accounts and API tokens are separate per region — use the same region here as on onetimesecret.com when you create your account and API token.

preferences.name: username
preferences.title: Username
preferences.description: Your Onetime Secret login name for this region. Optional — leave blank to create secrets anonymously. Required (with API token) for Recent Secrets.

preferences.name: apiToken
preferences.title: API token
preferences.description: Create an API token in your account on the regional site (e.g. uk.onetimesecret.com) and paste it here. Must match the selected region. Username and token are sent as HTTP Basic Auth — not stored by the extension beyond Raycast preferences.

commands.name: index
commands.title: Send One-Time Secret
commands.description: Stores a secret on One-Time Secret and gives back the shareable URL.

commands.name: send-from-clipboard
commands.title: Send from Clipboard
commands.description: Creates a one-time secret from the clipboard (3h lifetime, no passphrase) and copies the link.

commands.name: recent-secrets
commands.title: Recent Secrets
commands.description: Lists recent secrets for your account (requires username and API token in preferences).

tools.name: send-one-time-secret
tools.title: Send One-Time Secret
tools.description: Stores a secret on One-Time Secret and gives back the shareable URL.

evals.input: @one-time-secret store "santa is real" for 1 week. passcode: "christmas"

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
